raw_consumer_statement: On XX/XX/2024, my XXXX account was hacked. XXXX stole {$350.00} using my XXXX and CITI Bank accounts. Finally, XXXX XXXX  refunded my money {$350.00}. But CITI Bank refunded me {$350.00} first on XX/XX/2024 and finally the bank got my {$350.00} back on XX/XX/2024. This amount is just that big. I asked for a signature or proof of a legal document or contract but no one could give it to me. Why did Citibank take my money {$350.00}. I don't need to buy it at all and have no idea what it is? Why do I have to pay? Why can't banks protect consumers ' rights?
